Class Income

java.lang.Object
model.Income
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Income extends Object implements Serializable
Represents an income entry in the budget tracking system. This class encapsulates all relevant information about an income source, including its source, amount, date, description, and recurring status. Each income entry has a unique identifier generated upon creation. The class implements Serializable to allow for persistent storage.
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • Income

      public Income()
      Default constructor for serialization. Initializes a new income entry with a unique identifier.

    • Income

      public Income(String source, BigDecimal amount, LocalDate date, String description, boolean recurring, String recurringPeriod, int userId)
      Constructs a new income entry with the specified details.
      Parameters:
      source - The source of the income (e.g., "Salary", "Freelance")
      amount - The monetary amount of the income
      date - The date when the income was received
      description - A description of the income
      recurring - Whether the income is recurring
      recurringPeriod - The period of recurrence if recurring (e.g., "weekly", "monthly", "yearly")
      userId - The ID of the user associated with this income

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Gets the unique identifier of the income entry.
      Returns:
      The income's unique identifier

    • setId

      public void setId(String id)
      Sets the unique identifier of the income entry. Generally used only during deserialization.
      Parameters:
      id - The unique identifier to set

      public String getSource()
      Gets the source of the income.
      Returns:
      The income source

      public void setSource(String source)
      Sets the source of the income.
      Parameters:
      source - The new income source to set

    • getAmount

      public BigDecimal getAmount()
      Gets the monetary amount of the income.
      Returns:
      The income amount as a BigDecimal for precise currency calculations

    • setAmount

      public void setAmount(BigDecimal amount)
      Sets the monetary amount of the income.
      Parameters:
      amount - The new amount to set

    • getDate

      public LocalDate getDate()
      Gets the date when the income was received.
      Returns:
      The income receipt date

    • setDate

      public void setDate(LocalDate date)
      Sets the date when the income was received.
      Parameters:
      date - The new date to set

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Gets the description of the income.
      Returns:
      The income description

    • setDescription

      public void setDescription(String description)
      Sets the description of the income.
      Parameters:
      description - The new description to set

    • isRecurring

      public boolean isRecurring()
      Checks if the income is recurring.
      Returns:
      true if the income is recurring, false otherwise

    • setRecurring

      public void setRecurring(boolean recurring)
      Sets whether the income is recurring.
      Parameters:
      recurring - true if the income is recurring, false otherwise

    • getRecurringPeriod

      public String getRecurringPeriod()
      Gets the period of recurrence if the income is recurring.
      Returns:
      The period of recurrence (e.g., "weekly", "monthly", "yearly")

    • setRecurringPeriod

      public void setRecurringPeriod(String recurringPeriod)
      Sets the period of recurrence for recurring income.
      Parameters:
      recurringPeriod - The new recurrence period to set (e.g., "weekly", "monthly", "yearly")

    • getUserId

      public int getUserId()
      Gets the ID of the user associated with this income.
      Returns:
      The user ID

    • setUserId

      public void setUserId(int userId)
      Sets the ID of the user associated with this income.
      Parameters:
      userId - The new user ID to set

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the income entry. Includes id, source, amount, date, recurring status, and recurring period.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A string representation of the income entry
